description: An advanced operations course for learners who have completed Kubernetes Intermediate 1. Covers stateful workloads with StatefulSet, batch and node-level controllers (Job, CronJob, DaemonSet), Namespace-based multi-tenancy with ResourceQuota, RBAC access control, scheduling control with Affinity and Taints/Tolerations, NetworkPolicy traffic restrictions, Helm packaging, and cluster observability with Prometheus, Grafana, and log collection.
summary: An advanced operations course for learners who have completed Kubernetes Intermediate 1. Covers stateful workloads with StatefulSet, batch and node-level controllers (Job, CronJob, DaemonSet), Namespace-based multi-tenancy with ResourceQuota, RBAC access control, scheduling control with Affinity and Taints/Tolerations, NetworkPolicy traffic restrictions, Helm packaging, and cluster observability with Prometheus, Grafana, and log collection.
target_audience: Learners who have completed Kubernetes Intermediate 1 (ConfigMap/Secret, PV/PVC, Probes, rolling updates and rollback, requests/limits and HPA, Ingress) and want to broaden their real-world cluster operations skills.

lectures.title: StatefulSet and Stateful Workloads - Stable Identity and Ordered Lifecycle
objectives: Understand the requirements of stateful workloads that a Deployment cannot meet
Explain how a Headless Service assigns a stable network identity to each Pod and how ordered (sequential) startup and shutdown work
Deploy a StatefulSet that uses volumeClaimTemplates to automatically provision a dedicated PVC for each Pod

lectures.title: Job, CronJob, and DaemonSet - Batch, Scheduled, and Node-Level Workloads
objectives: Distinguish between a Job (run-to-completion) and a CronJob (recurring on a schedule) and understand their behavioral differences
Configure key fields such as completions, parallelism, backoffLimit, and schedule
Understand the use cases for DaemonSet — placing exactly one Pod on every node — and deploy all three controller types hands-on

lectures.title: Namespaces and Multi-Tenancy - Resource Isolation and Usage Limits
objectives: Understand how Namespaces logically partition a cluster and how to set up multi-tenancy
Apply ResourceQuota to cap the total resource consumption of a Namespace
Use LimitRange to enforce default values and upper/lower bounds on Pods and containers

lectures.title: RBAC and ServiceAccounts - Defining Who Can Do What
objectives: Understand the difference between authentication and authorization and the concept of role-based access control (RBAC)
Explain the structure of Role/ClusterRole and RoleBinding/ClusterRoleBinding for granting permissions
Assign least-privilege permissions to a ServiceAccount and verify access using kubectl auth can-i

lectures.title: Scheduling Control - Node Affinity and Taints/Tolerations
objectives: Understand how the scheduler places Pods on nodes and when you need to influence that placement
Express placement rules using nodeSelector and Node/Pod Affinity and Anti-Affinity
Configure Taints to repel Pods from a node and Tolerations to grant selective exceptions

lectures.title: NetworkPolicy - Controlling Pod-to-Pod Traffic and Default Deny
objectives: Understand the default allow-all cluster network model and why traffic restriction is necessary
Define allowed traffic using label-selector-based ingress and egress rules
Apply a default-deny policy and then practice allowing only specific communication paths with NetworkPolicy

lectures.title: Packaging Applications with Helm - Charts, Values, and Release Management
objectives: Understand the pain of managing repetitive manifests and the problems Helm solves
Explain the Chart structure (templates, values.yaml, Chart.yaml) and how template variable substitution works
Deploy, upgrade, and roll back a release using helm install, helm upgrade, and helm rollback

lectures.title: Monitoring and Logging - Prometheus, Grafana, and Log Collection
objectives: Understand why observing cluster and workload health matters and what roles metrics and logs each play
Explain the monitoring flow: Prometheus scraping metrics and Grafana visualizing them as dashboards
Deploy the monitoring stack, explore dashboards, and practice the concepts of Pod log collection
